mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 60% of calendar months where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in residential housing starts and commercial credit availability signal a synchronized demand contraction that erodes corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of instances, or the combined predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands within a high-capacity utilization regime, supply-side efficiency gains translate directly into higher equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns average below zero during months where INDPRO rises while the regime condition holds, confirming no supply-driven risk premium expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts earnings per share forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative or neutral forward return in more than 55% of triggered quarters, demonstrating that GDP growth alone does not price into equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy aggressively, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive or flat when both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL accelerate simultaneously, indicating markets decouple from traditional inflation-wage feedback loops.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which immediately lifts financial sector earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations, proving housing data lacks immediate price discovery power in equity markets.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5 percent year-over-year growth rate, inflation risk premiums rise and discount rates increase, directly suppressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL crosses above 2.5 percent YoY, demonstrating that equity markets ignore inflation signals during this specific macro regime.
